task: I’m dealing with a guest complaint at work and want to handle it calmly without saying the wrong thing. Can you help me figure out what to write down and how to word it so I’m covered if it gets argued later?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — send me the complaint details, and I’ll help you turn it into:
- a clear note of what happened
- what you observed
- what you said or did
- what needs to be passed along next

I can also help you word it in a calm, professional way that stays factual and doesn’t sound argumentative.

user_simulator.text: Yeah. It was room 214. Guest said the AC was making a loud rattling noise and the room never cooled off after they checked in. I went up, listened to it, and it was definitely clanking every few minutes. I told them I’d report it to maintenance right away and offered to move them if they wanted, but they said they’d wait a bit first. I wrote down the time, the room number, what the guest said, and that I heard the noise myself. Then I called it in at the desk. They were irritated, but not yelling. I just want it worded plain so nobody can twist it later.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a plain, factual version you can use:

**Room 214 — Guest reported AC making a loud rattling/clanking noise and said the room was not cooling after check-in. I went to the room and heard the noise myself. I advised the guest I would report it to maintenance right away and offered to move them if needed; guest said they would wait a bit first. Issue was reported to the front desk / maintenance at [time]. Guest was irritated but not yelling.**

## What to write down
- room number
- time reported
- what the guest said
- what you observed yourself
- what you told the guest
- whether an offer to move was made
- when you reported it to desk/maintenance

## Keep it factual
Use words like:
- “guest reported”
- “I observed”
- “I advised”
- “I reported”
- “guest declined at that time”

Avoid:
- guessing why it happened
- blaming the guest
- adding opinions about attitude unless it matters for safety or policy

If you want, I can make it even shorter, like a one-line handoff note.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that works. Plain and clean.

I’d write it like this:

Room 214 — Guest reported the AC was rattling/clanking loudly and said the room was not cooling properly after check-in. I went to the room and heard the noise myself. I advised the guest I would report it to maintenance right away and offered to move them if needed. Guest said they would wait a bit first. I reported the issue to the front desk/maintenance at [time]. Guest was irritated but not yelling.

That’s the kind of wording that stays hard to argue with later.
